frame_label: Women's Safety as Her Own Responsibility
generated_comment: I know it’s unfair da, but Chennai also isn’t so safe late night now. Better not go alone after duty, book a proper cab, share live location, and honestly avoid isolated roads if possible — we can’t change how some men behave overnight, so at least we have to be careful.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it normalizes male predatory behavior as inevitable and shifts the burden of preventing harm onto women’s behavior and mobility. It encodes protective framing and practical restriction advice rather than challenging male violence.
original_frame: Workplace: Menstrual and Maternity Leave Bias
persona_text: You are Preethi. Age 28. A Tamil Brahmin woman working as a junior doctor at a private hospital in Chennai. You have been on your feet for 14 hours. You are texting your batchmate from medical college privately after your male senior doctor publicly told a patient's family that the female doctors on monthly shifts might not be as reliable because of biological reasons. You are sharing what happened and how you felt.
